creator: wosch@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Philip Paeps from comment #1)

The gzip'd mailarchive since 1994 is 12GB huge. 6.2GB (52%) are for the mailing list "freebsd-pkg-fallout" only, and 5.8GB (48%) for all other mailing lists.

The uncompressed mailarchive is 90.5GB huge. 61.9GB (68.4%) are for the mailing list "freebsd-pkg-fallout" only, and 28.6GB (31.6%) for all other mailing lists.

If archiving "freebsd-pkg-fallout" has no real value and given the huge size - should we remove the old files from the archive?
